title: Factory and mock APIs must come from trusted driver API evidence
symptoms: Factory calls driver constructor with wrong keyword
Mock lacks a public method used by task
Device smoke test passes scan but runtime task fails
Generated code imports a MicroPython-lib runtime package but guesses its API from the package name
wrong_pattern: Guessing driver constructor or method names from package name only.
correct_pattern: Read driver .py, README, examples, or structured package API metadata before writing factory/mock. For driver.source=micropython_lib, use api_ref/readme/examples/docs/package metadata without vendoring source into firmware/lib.
fix_guidance: Extract main driver class from source when the driver source is downloaded.
Use README/example for default I2C address and constructor shape.
For MicroPython-lib runtime-only packages, require driver.api_ref or docs/readme/examples metadata before generating calls.
If API evidence is missing, return partial and request upstream package-guide metadata instead of copying source into firmware/lib or guessing.
Mock every public method used by generated tasks.
